description:
Implement global WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove Claude Code hooks that translate to jj workspace add/forget when the session repo is a jj repo, with a git-worktree fallback otherwise.

Design (grilled):
- Two writeShellApplication scripts in home-modules/settings/programs/claude-code.nix (jq/jj/git runtime inputs), wired into global settings.json WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove (no matchers).
- create: parse stdin JSON via jq; detect jj via 'jj --ignore-working-copy root' on cwd; jj -> mkdir -p parent + 'jj workspace add --name $worktree_name $worktree_path' (no -r) + echo path; non-jj -> 'git worktree add -b $worktree_name $path HEAD' with --detach fallback + echo path.
- remove: guarded on jj-detection; jj -> 'jj workspace forget $worktree_name' (idempotent) + defensive rm -rf; non-jj -> no-op.
- Accepted consequence: secondary jj workspaces have no own .git; raw git toplevel resolves to main repo; agents go jj-native.
- Docs: new docs/adr/0004-*, revise docs/agents/issue-tracker.md subagent-worktree section; no CONTEXT.md change.
- Reverses the documented 'subagents use git worktrees' convention; jj workspaces become the isolation primitive.
title: jj-workspace-backed Claude Code worktree hooks
